---
title: "LRP97: Negative-control outcome — letter sounds (L) -> receptive vocabulary (R)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

Tier-1 decoding-specificity mini-suite, design 1B (see
`notes/202607172330-tier1-decoding-specificity-spec.md`). A **negative-control outcome**
for the letter-sound slope (Lipsitch, Tchetgen Tchetgen & Cohen 2010,
[doi:10.1097/EDE.0b013e3181d61eeb]).

Under the revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`) letter-sound knowledge $LS$ has
descendants only $\{NW, PA, PS, WR\}$, so receptive vocabulary $RV$ is **not** a causal
descendant of $LS$: there is no $LS \to \cdots \to RV$ path. Any adjusted $L \to R$
association is therefore purely backdoor (through the shared parents $\{A, GA, HS, IG, IS,
SP\}$), and those backdoors run through the *same* parent set as the reading outcomes —
exactly the negative-control-outcome condition ("known not to be an effect of the primary
exposure", "subject to the same source of confounding").

::: {.callout-important title="What a null vs a non-null means"}
With the matched Tier-1 adjustment, the $L$ slope should be $\approx 0$ here while it is
clearly positive on the written-code outcomes ($W$ = [LRP101](../lrp-rli-mech-101), $N$ =
[LRP96](../lrp-rli-mech-096)). If $L$ *also* predicts receptive vocabulary, letter sounds
are behaving like a general-ability / teaching-dose marker and the letter → reading
association is suspect. This is a **falsification test**, not a mechanism.
:::

## Adjustment set

$\{G, A, HS, IS, SP\}$ plus baseline $R_\text{pre}$ — identical conditioning to the rest of
the Tier-1 panel so the negative-control forest is like-for-like. GA is unblockable; the
slope is an **adjusted association**.

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
